data_in.description: IM-MS lipidomics dataset (mzML, mzXML, or tabular feature table with m/z, retention time, and measured CCS values)
DTCCSN2 library for U13C labeled lipids from MobiLipid repository
R Markdown file from MobiLipid repository implementing CCS bias calculation

data_out.description: CCS bias assessment report containing per-lipid bias values, class-level bias statistics, and visualizations

domain_knowledge: CCS bias represents systematic deviation between measured and theoretical collision cross section values caused by instrumental calibration drift or ESI-source conditions in IM-MS.

The DTCCSN2 library uses universally labeled U13C lipid standards to establish reference CCS values, enabling internal standardization without requiring external calibration beyond vendor specifications.

Effective CCS bias assessment requires matching experimental lipid features to the reference library by both mass accuracy and chromatographic retention behavior; false matches inflate bias estimates.

CCS bias is typically expressed and aggregated at the lipid class level (e.g., PC, PE, TAG) because systematic bias patterns are often class-specific due to differences in ionization efficiency and charge distribution.

finding: MobiLipid performs CCS bias assessment through a R Markdown workflow that integrates a newly established DT CCS N2 library for U13C labeled lipids, enabling internal standardization-based bias calculation without requiring additional external calibration beyond vendor-specific requirements.
inputs: IM-MS lipidomics dataset (mzML, mzXML, or tabular feature table with m/z, retention time, and measured CCS values)
DTCCSN2 library for U13C labeled lipids from MobiLipid repository
R Markdown file from MobiLipid repository implementing CCS bias calculation
methodology_summary: Load IM-MS experimental data and DTCCSN2 reference library into R environment.
Match experimental lipid features to reference library entries by m/z, retention time, and lipid class annotation.
Calculate per-lipid CCS bias as measured CCS minus theoretical CCS value from library.
Aggregate bias metrics by lipid class and compute summary statistics (mean, SD, range, quartiles).
Validation: Report generated with per-class bias means and confidence intervals; bias values within expected instrument calibration tolerance (typically ±2–3%) indicate acceptable CCS quality.
missing_information: No changelog documenting version history, updates, or modifications to the MobiLipid repository and DTCCSN2 library

title: Reconstruct the CCS bias assessment component of the MobiLipid R Markdown workflow
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Load IM-MS lipidomics data and the DTCCSN2 library for U13C labeled lipid reference values into R. 2. Map detected lipid features to the DTCCSN2 reference library by matching mass-to-charge and retention time identifiers. 3. Calculate experimental-to-theoretical CCS bias for each matched lipid as the difference between measured and reference CCS values. 4. Aggregate bias metrics by lipid class and generate summary statistics (mean bias, standard deviation, range). 5. Generate bias assessment report with visualizations showing systematic CCS deviation patterns and class-level bias distribution.
